title: Portfolio Decarbonization Analysis
description: Schema for the portfolio-analysis report type — portfolio-scale decarbonization analysis across all analysis-ready assets in a Soapbox portfolio (Audette physics + Soapbox DCF engine). CLIENT-RENDER template: the server injects this object into <script id="report-data"> and templates/portfolio-analysis/layout-agent.html renders every section and chart from it (fill_report path, same as RSRA/decarb). Base fields are the Phase-5 keys documented in skills/portfolio-analysis/SKILL.md. The signature Asset-Prioritization quadrant renders from the prioritization[] block, which is folded from the Audette "<client> Asset Prioritization Sheet.xlsx" (Prioritization / Values and Cap Rates / Cost and Savings Splits / Emissions Splits sheets). Every section hides gracefully when its data is absent. 2 significant figures; energy kWh / kWh·m⁻²; currency via fmtCurrency ($M/$K).

client_name.description: Client name. Renders the header title as '<client_name> Portfolio'.

portfolio_id.description: Soapbox portfolio ID or name (identification only; not displayed).

report_date.description: Report date string (e.g. '2026-06-18'). Renders in the meta strip.

prepared_by.description: Preparer label for the meta strip. Defaults to 'Aris (Soapbox AI)' if omitted.

fund_list.description: Optional human-readable fund list for the header subline. If omitted, derived from fund_summary[].fund.

org_goal.description: Optional organizational sustainability goal statement. When set with portfolio_kpis.org_goal_pct, a 5th KPI tile renders.

org_goal_label.description: Short label for the org-goal KPI tile (e.g. 'Net Zero 2040'). Defaults to 'Goal'.

parameters.description: Run configuration. Percent fields accept either a decimal fraction (0.15) or an already-percent number (15) — the template normalizes. Renders the footer parameters line and IRR-hurdle references throughout.

irr_hurdle.description: Minimum IRR for a measure to be recommended (e.g. 0.15 or 15).

utility_escalation.description: Annual escalation applied to energy savings (e.g. 0.03 or 3).

exit_year_floor.description: Assets exiting before this year are moved to the floor date.

value_method.description: 'inclusive' = NOI uplift capitalised at exit cap + added to terminal CF; 'standalone' = IRR on savings only.

target_years.description: Emissions scenario target years. Drives the trajectory summary-table columns. Defaults to [2030, 2035, 2040, 2050].

executive_summary.description: Executive summary block. Accepts a plain string (rendered as narrative) or an object with headline + narrative. Hidden if absent.

headline.description: One-line factual takeaway. State magnitudes with numbers; NO hyperbolic/marketing adjectives ('massive', 'huge', 'game-changing', 'transformative').

narrative.description: Summary written as SEVERAL SHORT PARAGRAPHS separated by blank lines (\n\n) — one distinct point per paragraph (e.g. ¶ scope + headline economics, ¶ CRREM/carbon position, ¶ where the value vs the carbon sits, ¶ regulatory exposure, ¶ org-goal gap). Do NOT write one dense block. Plain, factual tone — no hyperbole. Define every acronym on first use, e.g. 'retro-commissioning (RCx)', 'gross floor area (GFA)', 'behind-the-meter (BTM)', 'building performance standard (BPS)'.

portfolio_kpis.description: Headline KPI tiles (Phase 4A). Section hides if none of the four core fields are present.

total_capex_net.description: Total net-of-IRA CapEx across all recommended measures, USD. Tile 1.

total_capex_gross.description: Total gross CapEx, USD (optional; not tiled).

value_creation.description: Total value creation at exit cap, USD. Tile 2 (accent green).

emissions_reduction_tco2.description: Total emissions reduction potential, tCO2e. Tile 3.

assets_above_hurdle.description: Count of assets with at least one recommended measure above the IRR hurdle. Tile 4 numerator.

assets_total.description: Total analyzed assets. Tile 4 denominator; also feeds the header asset count.

compliance_exposure.description: Σ compliance cost if no action, USD (only meaningful with BPS).

pathway_aligned_2035.description: Count of assets pathway-aligned at 2035 after measures (only with CRREM).

org_goal_pct.description: Percent of org_goal achieved under the IRR pathway. With org_goal set, renders a 5th tile.

prioritization.description: SIGNATURE BLOCK — one object per asset, folded from the Audette '<client> Asset Prioritization Sheet.xlsx' Prioritization sheet (columns confirmed against the 2026-06-18 Greystar sheet). Drives the Asset-Prioritization quadrant: X = pct_reduction (emissions-reduction potential), Y = value_creation ($, from the DCF — the sheet's 'Value Creation Impact'), bubble radius ∝ reduction_potential_tco2e, color = above_hurdle (or derived from irr vs parameters.irr_hurdle), quadrant dividers at the medians of X and Y. Rows missing pct_reduction OR value_creation are silently omitted from the plot (real sheets carry #N/A rows, e.g. assets with no decarb model or already past exit). Section hides unless >= 2 plottable points exist.

asset.description: Property Name (sheet col 1). Labels the point.

rank.description: Optional explicit priority rank; the sheet orders rows by Modeled Emissions descending.

eui_kwh_m2.description: Site EUI. The sheet reports EUI (kWh/ft2, col 3); convert to kWh·m⁻² for reporting (× 10.7639) or store as provided and label the unit.

ghgi_kg_m2.description: GHG intensity. Sheet col 4 is GHGi (kg/ft2); convert to kg·m⁻² for reporting.

reduction_potential_tco2e.description: '2050 Reduction possible (tCO2e)' — sheet col 5. Drives the quadrant bubble SIZE (the sheet's GAV column is a corrupted date-serial; do not size by GAV). HARD BOUND: a building cannot cut more than it emits — this MUST be <= modeled_emissions_tco2e. If the source cell is blank, corrupted, or exceeds baseline emissions, clamp to modeled_emissions_tco2e (or omit). Never emit a value larger than the asset's own baseline.

pct_reduction.description: '2050 % Reduction possible' — sheet col 6. Quadrant X-AXIS (%-savings scale). MUST be a FRACTION in [0,1], defined as reduction_potential_tco2e / modeled_emissions_tco2e (e.g. 0.30 = a 30% cut). NEVER a percent (do NOT emit 30) and NEVER > 1 (>100% reduction is impossible). Compute it from the two fields above rather than copying the sheet cell, and clamp to 1.0 if the arithmetic exceeds it.

residual_at_exit.description: 'Residual at Exit' — sheet col 7 (residual value/emissions at exit, USD or as modeled).

value_creation.description: 'Value Creation Impact' — sheet col 8, USD. Quadrant Y-AXIS. Comes from the DCF engine; may be negative (e.g. Union Tempe = -$2.1M).

irr.description: 'IRR on Incremental CapEx (w Asset Value)' — sheet col 9 (value-inclusive; matches parameters.value_method 'inclusive'). Used to derive above_hurdle when above_hurdle is absent. Accepts fraction or percent.

irr_ex_value.description: 'IRR on Incremental CapEX (w/o Asset Value)' — sheet col 10 (mostly blank in the source; standalone method).

gav.description: 'GAV' — sheet col 14, gross asset value USD. NOTE: in the 2026-06-18 Greystar sheet this column held corrupted Excel date-serials; validate before trusting, and never size the quadrant by it.

above_hurdle.description: Whether the asset's bundle meets the IRR hurdle. If omitted, the template derives it from irr vs parameters.irr_hurdle. Sets the point color (green = above, grey = below).

fund_summary.description: Fund-level rollup (Phase 4B). Section hides if empty. A 'Portfolio' totals row is appended when > 1 fund.

top_assets.description: Top-N assets by value creation (Phase 4C). Section hides if empty. Title auto-sizes to the array length. Rank defaults to array order.

pct_reduction.description: Fraction (0–1) of this asset's baseline emissions cut by its in-hold measures. MUST be a fraction, never a percent, never > 1.

measure_categories.description: Measure-category aggregate (Phase 4D). Renders a table with an inline CapEx bar per row and an 'All categories' totals row. Section hides if empty. $/tCO2 is computed as value_creation ÷ tco2_reduced.

programmatic_recommendation.description: Portfolio-scale sequenced program (Phase 4G) — the SAME recommended measures executed as a phased portfolio program rather than asset-by-asset. Renders the 'Portfolio-Scale Program & Economies of Scale' section (hidden if neither this nor scale_opportunities is present). Derive from the aggregation; every capex/reduction figure is engine-sourced, not invented.

thesis.description: 1-3 sentence portfolio-program thesis (section intro). Plain, factual.

phases.description: Sequenced phases (e.g. by CRREM-stranding urgency, capital-event timing, or measure family). Rendered as a table.

name.description: Phase label, e.g. 'Phase 1 — portfolio controls/RCx rollout'.

assets.description: Count of assets in this phase.

program.description: What the phase deploys across those assets.

capex_net.description: Aggregate net CapEx for the phase, USD (engine-sourced).

reduction_pct.description: Portfolio GHGI reduction the phase delivers (fraction 0-1 or percent; both render).

scale_opportunities.description: Economies-of-scale levers (Phase 4G) available BECAUSE this is a portfolio, not single assets — bulk procurement, clustered-contractor mobilization, aggregated solar/VNM or community solar, portfolio green loan / C-PACE, shared M&V subscription, IRA bonus stacking, GRESB/reporting leverage. Each must be grounded in the run data (which measures/assets/geographies it applies to); a quantified benefit needs a basis — mark assumed discounts (est.).

lever.description: e.g. 'Bulk HPWH procurement', 'Clustered-metro contractor mobilization', 'Aggregated VNM / community solar', 'Portfolio C-PACE'.

applies_to.description: Which assets/measures/geographies — e.g. '28 assets (LED)', '9 TX-metro assets'.

description.description: The opportunity and how scale unlocks it.

quantified_benefit.description: Est. savings/benefit with units, e.g. '~$0.4M procurement savings (est.)'. Leave '—' if not quantifiable; never invent a figure.

basis.description: Source or the explicit assumption behind the quantified benefit (e.g. '10% bulk discount assumption', 'IRA §48E domestic-content bonus').

emissions_trajectory.description: Portfolio carbon-intensity time series 2025-2050 (Phase 4E, only if include_crrem). Drives the 5-line curve (BAU + three deployment screens + CRREM) + the target-year summary table. Values are portfolio GFA-weighted kgCO2e/m2. Section hides if < 2 rows.

bau.description: Business-as-usual (no decarb capital), grey dashed line.

scenario_a.description: Scenario A — measures with irr_excl_exit >= hurdle (IRR excluding exit residual), blue line.

scenario_b.description: Scenario B — measures with irr_incremental >= hurdle (IRR including exit residual), green line.

scenario_c.description: Scenario C — B plus max solar (BTM self-consumption + VNM export at 80% owner), purple line.

scenario_d.description: Scenario D — Next Owner's Perspective: the B screen re-run at exit_year=2040 (deepest), amber line.

exit_price_protection.description: The seller's payoff on a short hold (Phase 4G / Scenario D): the buyer price chip that decarb avoids at the 2031 exit. Renders as a callout in the Portfolio-Scale section. Values may be $ (number → formatted) or a string (e.g. '25-50 bps'). Every figure needs a cited basis — do not invent.

headline.description: 1-2 sentence framing — decarb protects the exit price by removing the buyer's ACTUAL-BPS-fine and electrification price-chip levers. Do NOT claim a stranding/'brown-discount' price chip: CRREM stranding is a timing signal, never a capitalized haircut.

compliance_discount_avoided.description: The exit-price chip a buyer would apply for ACTUAL BPS fine exposure they would inherit (real jurisdiction fines), avoided by decarb. This is NOT driven by CRREM stranding or a generic 'brown discount' — stranding is never monetized. $0 unless the asset faces real fines. Number (USD) or string (e.g. '25 bps cap-rate') tied to the actual-fine basis.

electrification_reserve_avoided.description: Deferred gas→electric retrofit reserve a buyer would deduct from their bid, avoided. Number (USD) or string.

total.description: Total exit-price protection. Number (USD) or string.

basis.description: The cited assumption/source behind the figures (discount bps, reserve $/unit, comparable, etc.).

crrem_trajectory.description: CRREM pathway summary (only if include_crrem). Section hides unless current_intensity or stranding_today_2030 is present. Intensity fields in kgCO2e·m⁻².

stranding_today_2030.description: Assets stranding before 2030 with no action.

stranding_measures_2030.description: Assets stranding before 2030 with recommended measures.

excluded_note.description: Note on assets excluded from the pathway (estimated EUI).

bps_assets.description: Building Performance Standards exposure (only if include_bps). Section hides if empty. A warning callout with the summed annual fine renders when the total > 0.

basis.description: Penalty basis from the Regulations & Fines connector. 'eui' (DC BEPS, Denver) rows with a null annual_fine render as 'liable · unquantified' (need an EUI target), NOT $0. Omit and the renderer infers 'eui' from the jurisdiction name.

annual_fine.description: Projected annual fine if no action, USD. Use null (not 0) when liable but not yet quantified — e.g. an EUI-based standard with no EUI target supplied — so it renders 'needs EUI target' rather than a misleading $0. 0 means liable-but-currently-compliant.

fine_avoidance.description: Fine avoidance captured by measures, USD (100% landlord). null when unquantified.

assets.description: Asset-by-asset detail (Phase 5). Section hides if empty. CRREM Status and BPS Exposure columns auto-appear only when at least one row carries crrem_status / bps_exposure. Flag a row with flagged:true to mark a data point that needs follow-up (⚠).

pct_reduction.description: Fraction (0–1) of the asset's baseline emissions cut by its in-hold measures. MUST be a fraction, never a percent, never > 1.

irr.description: IRR as a DECIMAL fraction where 1.0 = 100% (e.g. 0.18 = 18%). Never a percent-number. The renderer multiplies by 100 for display.

crrem_status.description: 'aligned' | 'at risk' | 'stranded' (badged) or free text. Only if include_crrem.

bps_exposure.description: Annual fine (number → currency) or free text. Only if include_bps.

flagged.description: Data point needs follow-up → ⚠ marker (client-facing tooltip).

below_hurdle.description: Below-hurdle measures reference table. Section hides if empty. IRR shown in red (neg) for transparency.

barrier.description: Why it misses (e.g. 'NNN tenant-metered — low LL capture').

deferred.description: Measures deferred due to retrofit lead-time vs hold period. Section hides if empty.

all_measures.description: Flat list of every measure across all assets (recommended + below-hurdle + deferred). Consumed by the XLSX companion (Phase 3E) for analyst verification; not rendered by this HTML template. Shape per SKILL.md Phase 3E.

asset_source_data.description: Raw per-asset inputs (Audette data + DCF parameters). Consumed by the XLSX companion (Phase 3E) for analyst verification; not rendered by this HTML template. Shape per SKILL.md Phase 3E.

data_quality.description: Client-facing data-quality summary and confidence dots. EXTERNAL DELIVERABLE: no internal-process language (no 'verifier', 'finding id', 'Gate', 'adjudicated', 'phase'). Do NOT include an internal conflict/findings table. Section hides unless summary or items is present.

summary.description: Client-facing prose summary: how many assets use metered/measured EUI vs. peer-benchmark estimates, and the basis of confidence. No internal-process terms.

items.description: Dotted confidence items — client-facing text only (cite the data source, not the internal QA step).

text.description: Client-facing confidence note. No internal-process terms.

methodology.description: Methodology prose for the appendix, written as SEVERAL SHORT PARAGRAPHS separated by blank lines (\n\n) — group by topic (e.g. ¶ data sources, ¶ LL/TT capture, ¶ economics engine, ¶ CRREM, ¶ BPS, ¶ escalation & uncertainty). Not one dense block. Plain factual tone, no hyperbole; define acronyms on first use. Section hides if this and sources are both absent.

sources.description: Citations for the collapsible sources block. URLs are validated (http/https only) and open in a new tab.
